title: BoysBet — peer-betting app sketch, Dec 2023
html:
TL;DR

  • New Year's Eve 2023 product sketch: a friends-only betting app with three core mechanics — tournament style, integrated with Discord, acts as quasi-escrow for bets between friends.
  • Naming brainstorm captured: StakeWrld, WagerWrld, Stakespace — note the recurring "Wrld" suffix from Yahir's wrld brand suffix pattern (also in 2024 07 12 wrld side quests).
  • Nichest application: bets within a friend group + 1:1 bets + group bets — same scope already named in the peer wager app concept and the 2024 12 28 ideas entry.
  • Open product question logged: "How to validate who wins in private gaming bets. Eg rocket league, 2k, COD." — outcome-verification is the unsolved problem.

Key quote

"How to validate who wins in private gaming bets. Eg rocket league, 2k, COD."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Third and clearest articulation of the peer wager app concept — earlier pre-imagery in 2024 12 28 ideas ("App to bet within friend") and even older in scattered notes. Three independent appearances justify the existing concept page.
  • The "Wrld" suffix is now confirmed across multiple separate ventures: WagerWrld (this), Wrld Side Quests, BroMenu's tone — earns its own wrld brand suffix concept page.
  • Outcome-verification problem (esports-results-from-friends) is the same blocker as Rankett's "is there a way to see what reaches come from AI" question in 2026 01 02 diddles call — Yahir consistently runs into measurement-honesty as the engineering bottleneck.

Open questions

  • Did StakeWrld get any prototype love, or stayed at naming?
  • Discord-integration as escrow — viable on the Discord ToS side, or always too risky?

Wikilinks
